MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Lamping appeared by zoom for William Kwong (Petitioner).
Lillian Ng appeared by zoom for Kiernan Kwong (Administrator).
Edward Koplowitz appeared by telephone for Kiernan Kwong, Linda Kwong and Sandra Lee (Beneficiaries).

The Court stated that the only issue is in the language regarding the stipulation to have some orders from the Court vacated. Mr. Lamping stated that there are some interim orders that were entered, that they have resolved the issues and the agreement would conflict with the previous orders and that there is a stipulation regarding those orders. Ms. Ng and Mr. Koplowitz stated that they are in favor of approval of the settlement agreement and granting of the petition. The Court granted the Petition to Approve Settlement (subject to review of the order). The Court orders that the orders of May 9, 2019 and December 11, 2019 are vacated. The motion to enforce automatic stay on appeal that is currently set for October 7, 2020 is off calendar at Mr. Lamping's request.

MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James P. Lamping for and with William Kwong.
Edward A. Koplowitz for Kierin Kwong, Linda Kwong and Sandra Lee.
Lillian E. Ng for Kierin Kwong.

Mr. Lamping addressed housekeeping issue that the Petition for Final Distribution cannot move forward due to appeal. Mr. Koplowitz stated that William Kwong wanted to buy and live in the property. Ms. Ng stated the end game is to settle the estate and sell the property to William Kwong. Mr. Lamping stated that any record of William Kwong continuing to live at the property is not true, that his client wants to see the property rented, that the Administrator cannot rent it because it does not meet health and safety conditions for a rental, and proposes to have a written inspection report by David Milazzo to verify needed repairs. Mr. Koplowitz stated that he does not know if there are code compliance issues for lack of habitability, that William Kwong doesn't know about real estate, and that 3/4 of expenses will fall on his clients while 100% benefit goes to William Kwong. Ms. Ng stated it would be an impossible task to get assessment of repair costs in a week.

9:46AM: The Court send counsel/party to hallway to meet and confer on a plan.
11:15AM: The matter is re-called.

Mr. Koplowitz reported the parties' stipulation for the Administrator to obtain an inspector/contractor to inspect and provide a report to Mr. Lamping regarding habitability for rental of the property, the inspector can either bid to work on any needed repairs for find another contractor to bid, Mr. Lamping has the right to have his own inspector, if needed the parties will go back to one of the mediators, they will make the property rentable at the estate's expense, and request a status conference with the court in 45 days.

The Petition for Final Distribution is off calendar.

The Petition for Instructions and The Petition for Order Suspending Administrator, etc., are continued to April 6, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Koplowitz to provide a proposed order regarding the stipulation for property inspection and continuance.

Off Calendar May-09-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lillian Ng for Kieran Kwong (Administrator).
Edward Koplowitz for Kieran kwong, Linda Kwong and Sandra Lee (Beneficiaries).
Jim Lamping for and with William Kwong (Petitioner).

Mr. Lamping provided history, stated that they had mediation and had a settlement agreement, that he was then told that his Client committed a $300,000.00 breach, that he filed a petition to enforce the settlement agreement using the original numbers that were provided and that Counsel then filed a competing petition for approval of the settlement agreement. Mr. Koplowitz stated that all parties signed the settlement agreement and that it should be approved. Mr. Lamping stated that they are now asking for additional money/additional damages that his Client can't come up with, that the reality of it is that the agreement collapses and that he is asking the Court to approve the settlement with the original amount of money. The Court stated that if the parties can not work out their difference as to how language should be applied, they can go back to the Mediator. The Petition to Enforce Settlement Agreement, etc., Petition for Letters of Administration, Petition for Suspension of Powers and Removal of Executor, etc., and Petition for Order Approving Settlement Agreement, etc. are under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.
